items.question: True Positive (TP) means:
items.explanation: TP counts correct detections of the positive class — true label 1 and prediction 1.

items.question: False Positive (FP) means:
items.explanation: FP is a false alarm — the model predicted positive but the true label was negative.

items.question: For sklearn confusion_matrix with labels [0, 1], TN is at:
items.explanation: Row 0 is actual class 0; column 0 is predicted 0 — true negatives.

items.question: TPR (recall) equals:
items.explanation: TPR is the fraction of actual positives correctly flagged — uses the actual-positive row.

items.question: TP + FP + TN + FN equals:
items.explanation: Each example falls into exactly one cell — the four counts partition the evaluation set.

items.question: A confusion matrix is most appropriate for:
items.explanation: The matrix compares discrete predicted labels to discrete true labels at one cutoff — a classification evaluation tool.
